event.summary: Entered validation with a deterministic command, instruction, graph, and device-impact plan.

acceptance.proof: A dependency-free closed data contract exposes UDP port 16571 and the seven exact LSLC-002I destination spellings in documented order, including the unusual FF08113D token unchanged.

Every destination exposes only its static documented spelling and source-parenthesized presentation fact; it performs no address parsing, correction, normalization, classification, allocation, or operational interpretation.
The crate status and ownership declaration name the inert documented discovery-destination data contract while continuing to deny discovery runtime, sockets, endpoint selection, and interoperability.

No sockets, interface enumeration, multicast joins, broadcast sends, endpoint selection, retries, clocks, currentness, reachability/interoperability claims, runtime activation, dependencies, unsafe/FFI, devices, private artifacts, or Manifold authority are added.

document.commit_policy: Commit the closed data contract and its tests separately from lifecycle acceptance and push preparation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the exact LSLC-002J inert data contract and preserve runtime exclusions.
Synchronize the public contract and non-scope description.
Review public/private routing; no shared-skill mutation expected.
Review evidence-role and closed-activation boundaries; no shared-skill mutation expected.

non_scope: Address parsing, validation, normalization, correction, IP-family or multicast-scope classification, binary representation, deduplication, selection, policy, or endpoint semantics.
Sockets, interface enumeration, multicast joins, broadcast sends, discovery runtime, retries, clocks, currentness, reachability, interoperability, or activation.
Dependencies, features, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts, implementation-source inspection, black-box observation, or Manifold authority.
document.objective: Add the smallest dependency-free, allocation-free, source-only data contract justified by LSLC-002I for the documented protocol-110 discovery UDP port and exact displayed destination inventory, preserving every spelling and presentation fact without acquiring address or runtime semantics.
outputs: Closed Rust data inventory for documented port and destination spellings.
Exact-order, exact-spelling, allocation-free and public-ownership regression tests.
Public routing/architecture/validation documentation, Standard receipt, and exact-ID executed no-force feature-push receipt.
